proven.claim: PROVE: transitionCount is bounded — it cannot exceed the number of assertions.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 86
; PROVE: transitionCount is bounded — it cannot exceed the number of assertions.
; Each assertion contributes at most 1 to transitionCount.
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const counted_asserts Int)    ; asserts with referencedVars >= 2
(declare-const transition_count Int)
; Code: asserts is a filtered list, length >= 0
(assert (>= num_asserts 0))
; Code: counted_asserts is a subset of asserts
(assert (>= counted_asserts 0))
(assert (<= counted_asserts num_asserts))
; Code: transitionCount = counted_asserts (one increment per qualifying assert)
(assert (= transition_count counted_asserts))
; Negate: transitionCount > num_asserts
(assert (> transition_count num_asserts))
(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVE: when all assertions have < 2 referenced variables, transitionCount = 0.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 86
; PROVE: when all assertions have < 2 referenced variables, transitionCount = 0.
; Models the partition: single_var_count + transition_count = num_asserts
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const single_var_count Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
; At least one assertion exists (past early-return)
(assert (>= num_asserts 1))
; Code: every assertion has referencedVars < 2 → all go to single_var_count
(assert (= single_var_count num_asserts))
; Code: partition — every assert lands in exactly one bucket
(assert (= (+ single_var_count transition_count) num_asserts))
(assert (>= transition_count 0))
; Negate: transitionCount > 0 despite all assertions being single-variable
(assert (> transition_count 0))
(check-sat)
